meaning: Movement effects applied to text or objects within a presentation slide
definition: Movement effects applied to text or objects within a presentation slide
example: Each bullet point appeared with a fade animation.

meaning: Techniques used to involve and interest the audience during a presentation
definition: Techniques used to involve and interest the audience during a presentation
example: Quizzes and polls improve audience engagement during webinars.

meaning: A short piece of information highlighted with a dot or symbol in a list
definition: A short piece of information highlighted with a dot or symbol in a list
example: Keep bullet points concise for better audience readability.

meaning: A person who shares the role of presenting alongside the main presenter
definition: A person who shares the role of presenting alongside the main presenter
example: The marketing director joined as co-presenter for the product launch webinar.

meaning: The graphical representation of information and data
definition: The graphical representation of information and data
example: Effective data visualisation makes complex trends easy to understand.

meaning: A printed or digital document distributed to audience members during a presentation
definition: A printed or digital document distributed to audience members during a presentation
example: Participants received a handout summarising the key points.

meaning: A visual representation of information or data using graphics
definition: A visual representation of information or data using graphics
example: The infographic summarised the survey results in a clear format.

meaning: The most important point or lesson from a presentation or discussion
definition: The most important point or lesson from a presentation or discussion
example: The key takeaway is that early planning reduces project risks.

meaning: A written record of what was discussed and decided in a meeting
definition: The written record of a meeting.
example: The secretary shared the meeting minutes with all participants.

meaning: An interactive feature in a message or meeting allowing participants to vote on options
definition: An interactive feature in a message or meeting allowing participants to vote on options
example: The host launched a live poll to gauge audience opinions.

meaning: The person delivering a presentation or leading a meeting
definition: The person delivering a presentation or leading a meeting
example: The presenter spoke clearly and maintained eye contact with the camera.

meaning: A display mode showing the speaker's notes and upcoming slides during a presentation
definition: A display mode showing the speaker's notes and upcoming slides during a presentation
example: He used presenter view so he could see his notes while the audience saw the slides.

meaning: A detailed timeline of events during a presentation or event
definition: A detailed timeline of events during a presentation or event
example: The event coordinator shared the run of show with all speakers.

meaning: A set of slides used in a presentation, typically created with software
definition: A set of slides used in a presentation, typically created with software
example: She prepared a polished slide deck for the investor pitch.

meaning: Key ideas or facts a speaker wishes to convey during a presentation
definition: Key ideas or facts a speaker wishes to convey during a presentation
example: He prepared talking points to stay on track during the interview.
